name: Electrical Workers Local 130
description_short: IBEW Local 130 is a member-owned credit union serving electrical workers and their families in the Greater New Orleans area since 1900.
description_long:
IBEW Local 130 is the New Orleans chapter of the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers, established on June 11, 1900. For over 125 years, the organization has served as both a trade union representing electricians and a credit union for its members. The organization operates within the Greater New Orleans region and has built its reputation on advocating for fair wages, safer working conditions, and professional development in the electrical trades.

The credit union provides financial services to IBEW Local 130 members and their families. Based on the website navigation and structure, members can access credit union services including share accounts and other member financial products. The organization also facilitates dues payments for active and retired members, indicating a full-service financial operation for its constituent population.

What distinguishes IBEW Local 130 is its dual purpose as both a labor union and credit union serving a specific professional community. The organization combines financial services with apprenticeship training, union representation, and workplace advocacy. The credit union structure ensures member-owned, not-for-profit operations that benefit electricians rather than external shareholders.

The main caveat is that membership appears restricted to IBEW electricians, apprentices, retirees, and their families—this is not a community bank open to the general public. The website provides limited details about specific credit union products, rates, or terms. Prospective members should contact the organization directly to understand full eligibility requirements and available financial services beyond dues payment and basic share accounts.
services: Credit union membership for IBEW electricians and families
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit credit union structure ensuring returns benefit electricians rather than external investors
Over 125 years of established history and reputation in the Greater New Orleans electrical trades community
Serves electricians at all career stages: apprentices, active workers, retirees, and their families
Integrated with union representation and apprenticeship training, providing comprehensive member support
Online dues payment system available for member convenience
Affiliated with the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers, a major national labor organization
cons: Membership appears limited to IBEW electricians and their families—not open to the general public
Website provides minimal detail about specific credit union products, rates, terms, or loan offerings
Limited online information about account types, minimum balances, APYs, or other financial product details
No clear information about whether non-members can access any services or products
Website does not detail credit union insurance coverage, investment options, or other advanced services
best_for: Active IBEW Local 130 union electricians seeking member-owned financial services and union support
Apprentices in the IBEW electrical trade training program needing accessible financial services
Retired IBEW electricians managing pensions and maintaining financial relationships with the union
diagnosis: IBEW Local 130 is best suited for licensed and apprentice electricians in the Greater New Orleans area who want member-owned, not-for-profit financial services aligned with their trade union. The main caveat is that this is a membership-restricted credit union with limited online transparency about specific products and services—prospective members must contact the organization directly to confirm eligibility and understand available offerings.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Electrical Workers Local 130 — NCUA-insured credit union in METAIRIE, LA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
